channel.title: Travel Australia on Hua Tuo Studio
channel.description: Recent content in Travel Australia on Hua Tuo Studio

item.title: 2026 Trips and Events
item.description:
A summary of weekend trips taken and events that we have attended during 2026, for memory keeping.

  • Feb: Bateman’s and Malua Bay 3 days w/ B&T
  • Shepherd’s Lookout beautiful place! w/ B&T
  • March: National Multicultural Festival w/ B&T
  • March: Canberra Balloon Spectacular w/ B&T
  • March: Canberra Day fireworks - Sky Fire 3.14 8:30pm w/ B&T
    • 7:55 pm: RAAF F-35A Lightning II flyover
  • March: Enlighten Festival Canberra 2026 w/ B&T
  • March: short trip to Sydney (work)
  • March: Blue Range Barry and Tom
  • Haig Park Village Markets, Sunday 8:00am to 2:00pm. Dogs are welcome. Dog agility training park next to it. w/ B&W
  • Epic farmers market - a lovely place to walk with your dog and people. w/ B&T
  • Experience Breathing Space, an evocative sound installation created for the Garden of Australian Dreams at the National Museum of Australia. w/ B&T
    • Composed by renowned musician Genevieve Lacey, Breathing Space pays homage to the deep time and ecological abundance of our continent, the ancient, vibrant cultures of First Nations Australians and the waves of migration that have shaped the land.
    • Wander through the Garden and discover sounds and melodies that change subtly, hour by hour, day by day, season by season. Whenever and wherever you are in the Garden, you will hear something new.
  • Saturday 2026-03-21 Harvest Day Out 2026 Lanyon Homestead, 10-3pm w/ Tom
  • 2026-03-27-29 Japan StrEat Party w/ T&B Barry and Bron
  • April: short trip to Sydney w/ Tom
  • late April: Bendora Arboretum ACT beautiful picnic w/ B&T Barry and Bron
  • May: Halls market - very nice outing. Dogs are welcome. w/ B&T
  • May: Mother’s Day Classic walk w/ B&W
  • July: Winter in the woods National Arboretum Canberra w/ B&Barry
  • July: Christmas in July w/ B&Barry
  • September: Gold coast (work)

item.title: 2025 Trips and Events
item.description:
A summary of weekend trips taken and events that we have attended during 2025, for memory keeping.

  • January: Bangalee & Shoalhaven Heads 3 days w/ B&W
  • March: National Multicultural Festival w/ B&W
  • Haig Park Village Markets, Sunday 8:00am to 2:00pm. Dogs are welcome. Dog agility training park next to it. w/ B&W
  • Corin Dam - National park, no dogs allowed. w/ B&W
  • Experience Breathing Space, an evocative sound installation created for the Garden of Australian Dreams at the National Museum of Australia. w/ B
    • Composed by renowned musician Genevieve Lacey, Breathing Space pays homage to the deep time and ecological abundance of our continent, the ancient, vibrant cultures of First Nations Australians and the waves of migration that have shaped the land.
    • Wander through the Garden and discover sounds and melodies that change subtly, hour by hour, day by day, season by season. Whenever and wherever you are in the Garden, you will hear something new.
  • May: RSPCA Million Paws Walk w/ B&GM
  • Halls market - very nice outing. Dogs are welcome. w/ B&W
  • Little Burley Market - a lovely place to walk with your dog and people. w/ B
  • June: Malua Bay for the long weekend w/ B&W
  • July: Sydney w/ JB
  • Young’s cherry festival w/ T&B
  • Dec: Sydney w/ T&B&Ming
  • Dec: NYE fireworks w/ B&T

item.title: 2024 Trips and Events
item.description:
A summary of trips taken and events that we have attended during 2024, for memory keeping.

  • Visit Goulburn - day trip w/ B&W
  • Visit Bungendore - day trip w/ B&W
  • Visit Lake George w/ B&W
  • New Year’s Eve 2024 firework w/ B&BB
  • Floriade w/ B
  • Balloon Festival w/ B
  • National Multicultural Festival w/ B
  • Christmas Picnic at Uriarra East w/ B, W&BB
  • Dec: NYE fireworks w/ B

item.title: Rodney's Nursery & Garden Center
item.description:
If you are a Canberran and you enjoy gardening and growing vegetables, you should know about Rodney’s nursery and his café. Ever since my mother moved to Canberra in March this year, I have been wanting to take her here for a meal. It has been raining for the past few weeks, and today it finally cleared up. The three of us wander here for lunch.

The garden café was already quite busy at around 11:00 on a Sunday morning. Although it was a cold winter morning, the sun was out, and sitting by the outdoor electric heater, it didn’t feel cold at all.

item.title: 2023 Trips and Events
item.description:
A summary of trips taken and events that we have attended during 2023, for memory keeping.

  • 20230520 - Sydney Manly w/ B&GM
  • 20230924-25 - Malua Bay w/ B&W
    • https://www.travelnuity.com/dog-friendly-batemans-bay/
    • https://www.esc.nsw.gov.au/residents/pets/dog-friendly-beaches-and-areas

item.title: Travel Checklist
item.description:
Life is simpler with a checklist. This is what I use before my travels:

Travel Documents

  • Check all your travel documentations carefully and contact your travel agencies immediately if you have any queries or problems,
  • It’s a good idea to have separate copies of all your documents (including passports) to carry and leave a copy of your itinerary with a relative or friend at home,
  • All names on the booking match passport names of all travellers,
  • Frequent flyer number has been advised and used in all bookings.

Passports

  • Check that your passport and visas are in order. Most countries require a minimum of 6 months’ validity on your passport (from your return date). If you are travelling on a non-Australian passport then you must carry correct re-entry documentation.
  • Check that you have the correct visa and passport type for your destination

Flight confirmation

Although most airlines do not require you to reconfirm, still, check your flight schedule at least 24 hours prior to departure. Airlines have the right to change flight times and dates without notice. Download the airline’s app and turn on notification.

item.title: Travel Bucket List
item.description:
• Soak in the Pamukkale thermal pools, Turkey

  • Explore the fascinating Salar de Uyuni salt flats, Bolivia

  • Find paradise on Palawan Island, the Philippines

  • See the spectacular Northern Lights

  • Discover the “temple that is a city” at Angkor Wat in Cambodia

  • Scale Japan’s highest peak, Mount Fuji

  • Take a trip into the wilderness on an African safari

  • Ascend the arch of Sydney Harbour Bridge - read my story here

item.title: Road Trip WA
item.description:
24-25, March 2016, Easter. Another year and another Easter, time goes so fast. Three of us has decided to take a road trip to the Western Australia’s Southern Forest Region. We all knew the region pretty well so the trip was roughly planned and the accommodation was booked a few days before the trip.

The Itinerary

Day 1

  • Leave Perth

  • Rest at Bunbury (1.5hrs), change driver

  • Take the National Route 1 from Bunbury to Walpole, rest at Bridgetown and Manjimup

item.title: Sydney Harbour Bridge Climb
item.description:
Sydney Bridge Climb - the experience

John has bought me a ticket for a full 3 hours of Sydney Harbour Bridge Climb for my birthday this year (thank you John!! 💕 ). I have to say that I have always thought the bridge climb is an overpriced tourist thing. Why would I ever do that if I live here?! :) Nevertheless, I was excited to wake up at 3am to get ready for the bridge climb.
